headline: Mayor Declares All Potholes 'Art Installations,' Demands Admission Fees To View Them
summary: 'It's about perspective,' the mayor said. 'These potholes represent our collective hope.'
fullBody: Mayor McArt declared all potholes 'urban art installations.' 'They represent the city's journey,' the mayor said. Residents are charged $5 to view them. 'I'm not paying to see a pothole,' one resident said. 'It's art,' the mayor said. The potholes have been named. 'This is 'Aspiration,'' the mayor said, pointing to a crater. 'This is 'Despair.''' The potholes are still there. They are still potholes.
